contest.title: 2025-2026 ICPC Asia Taichung Regional Contest (Unrated, Online Mirror, ICPC Rules, Teams Preferred)

sources.notes: Cut a circular cake by a line so all strawberries remain on one side and the smaller piece is maximized.
Chinese statement mirror used to confirm geometry and constraints.
No detailed editorial was located during this pass; convex-hull/support-line reduction is self-derived from the half-plane condition.

annotation.summary: Find the largest possible smaller circular segment after cutting a disk while keeping all points in the other piece.
annotation.constraints: n up to 2e5; only the convex hull of strawberries can matter.
annotation.core_idea: A valid cut is a line whose one closed half-plane contains the convex hull of all strawberries. To maximize the smaller circular segment, place such a supporting/separating line as close to the center as possible. If a line through the center can keep all points on one side, the answer is half the circle; otherwise use the closest supporting line of the convex hull and convert its distance from the center to a circular-segment area.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) for convex hull plus rotating/edge scan.

solution_variants.name: convex-hull-support-line
solution_variants.summary: Compute the closest valid supporting/separating line for the point convex hull, then evaluate segment area.

tags.evidence: The whole solution is a planar geometry reduction to support lines and circular segments.
Only the convex hull of strawberry points constrains feasible cuts.
tags.description: Use the convex hull of planar points for geometry optimization or feasibility.
tags.created_reason: Convex hull is a major geometry subtopic needed for precise search.

tags.evidence: The area computation is a formula after the geometric reduction, not a prefix transformation.
